name: Occipital Lobe
location: Posterior-most region of cerebral cortex situated above the tentorium cerebelli
description: The primary visual processing hub of the mammalian brain, transforming retinotopic signals from the LGN into detailed visual perception.
functions: Primary visual feature extraction (Striate Cortex V1)
Dorsal spatial stream and ventral object recognition stream splitting
clinicalImportance: Bilateral PCA occlusion leads to cortical blindness with preservation of pupillary light reflexes.
researchNotes: Retinotopic mapping shows point-to-point topographical projection of retinal receptive fields on V1.
interestingFacts: V1 contains nearly 140 million neurons in an area no larger than a credit card.
